title: Fix file path resolution
body:
This pull request makes a few changes:

1. It fixes the single file case where a `yield` should be used instead of a `return` - the difference seems to be that the former results in an empty generator evaluation when a single file is provided.
2. Absolute path resolution has been added for easier identification of file locations if the specified file is not found.
3. A more descriptive error message.

step_plan.summary: Fix corpus file path handling so single-file inputs iterate correctly in generator-based flows, and improve debuggability by resolving paths to absolute locations and emitting clearer “not found” errors. Updates focus on corpus path resolution/listing and the trainer’s consumption of the resulting iterable.

steps.operation: Update corpus path resolution to return an absolute, normalized path and raise a more descriptive error when resolution fails
steps.reason: `get_file_path` is the canonical entry point for turning either a dotted corpus reference or a filesystem path into a concrete location. Normalizing to an absolute path improves debuggability and ensures downstream error messages can point to the exact attempted location when the file/package is not found.
steps.side_effects: May change behavior/expectations for callers/tests that asserted relative paths or compared paths as raw strings; error message text/contents may change and require test updates. Any downstream code that joins paths onto the returned value should be checked to ensure it doesn’t double-resolve or incorrectly concatenate absolute paths.

steps.operation: Fix single-file corpus listing to produce a consistent iterable (use `yield` rather than `return` in generator-style logic), and validate existence with absolute path in errors
steps.reason: The PR indicates the single-file code path currently uses `return` where `yield` is required, which can cause empty iteration when callers treat the result as a generator/iterable. This function is also the right place to enforce consistent directory-vs-file handling: resolve to absolute paths, verify file existence, and emit actionable errors that include the resolved location and expected corpus extension(s).
steps.side_effects: Could change ordering/type of the returned object (e.g., generator vs list) depending on the existing implementation; callers relying on concrete lists may need minor adjustment. Improved validation may cause earlier failures (now raising on missing/invalid inputs instead of silently returning nothing), which can surface new errors in existing integrations.

steps.operation: Adjust trainer training loop to consume corpus file iterables robustly and propagate enriched corpus resolution errors
steps.reason: This trainer is the primary consumer of `list_corpus_files`/`load_corpus`. Ensuring it iterates over the corpus file iterable (including the single-file case) without accidentally short-circuiting and that it surfaces the improved, absolute-path-based error messages will satisfy the acceptance criteria that single-file training produces non-empty iteration and missing files produce actionable diagnostics.
steps.side_effects: Behavior of exceptions surfaced from training may change (more descriptive messages, possibly different exception types). If this function previously assumed a list, it may need to avoid multiple passes over a generator (e.g., avoid calling `len()` or iterating twice), which can affect logging/debug output and any related tests.
